column17:
Kit Overview: CRIT DPS who eats SP whole
Archer's Skill makes him enter the Circuit Connection state and extends his turn. In this state, he consumes 2 SPs to launch a powerful ST attack. This attack is considered Skill damage, Archer will exit this state after 5 Uses / Manually Ending / Run out of SP. Using this ability repeatedly will also greatly increase Skill damage.

In battle, Archer increases the SP cap by 2. Whenever allies attack enemies, Archer consumes 1 Charge to launch an FUA, and gains 1 SP. Archer's Ultimate deals massive ST to an enemy and grants 2 Charge. Additionally, entering battle or using his Technique will also grant 1 Charge.

Synergies:
✦ Supports that can generate Skill Points frequently or do not use much SPs themselves.
✦ Supports with good damage amplification, action advance, etc.

Tips: 
✦ Make sure Archer has as many Skill Points as possible before he enters the Circuit Connection state again.
column45:
✦ If you like a character, pull them! Archer will be given out for free during version 3.4, so you are likely to get him regardless.

✦ Archer is a great DPS for low-target content, and he is a great pick if you lack DPS of this archetype. However he will struggle in AoE situations such as Pure Fiction due to the nature of the Hunt path.

✦ Archer's teams are also very flexible, and can work with variety of supports. This makes him quite easy to play.
column71: Archer prefers a hypercarry setup with characters who can generate SPs quickly or do not consume much SP.

column59:
Sparkle increases the max amount of Skill Points while also generating a lot of SPs in a short burst, which makes her the most important support for Archer.

E1 will refund 2 SPs for 3 Skill attacks, this occur once a turn. This potentially extends Circuit Connection state and increases Archer's damage output.

E2 makes Archer's Ultimate reduce Quantum RES and apply Quantum weakness on the attacked enemy. This is a very solid damage increase.

column71: No Spork? - S1 Sunday is really wanted

column17:
Kit Overview: Ultimate & Follow-Up Attack DPS that thrives on team's attack frequency
Feixiao launches a FUA after using her skill and when an ally attacks. For every 2 ally attacks, a point of [Flying Aureus] is gained, storing up to 12 points. Activating her Ultimate consume 6 points, unleashing a strong Single-Target attack.

Her Ultimate counts as both FUA damage and Ultimate damage. It can deal Toughness Damage regardless of Weakness Type with increased Toughness Reduction against unbroken targets, for broken targets, increase DMG dealt. FInally, buff self Crit DMG% and ATK% by a good amount.

Synergies:
✦ Supports focused on buffing Crit DPS. 
✦ Action Advance/Speed buffers to the team more turns.
✦ Allies that attack frequently charge Feixiao's Ultimate more quickly.

Tips:
✦ You can use the weaker attack type during Feixiao's Ultimate as damage control. By keeping an enemy's HP low, you can finish them of with her Skill to charge more points for the next wave.
column45:
✦ If you like the character, get them!

✦ Feixiao is a flexible, strong Hunt DPS. She is a worthwhile pick up if you want a DPS who can help with low-target content. 

✦ She is also a strong option in Pure Fiction due to her ability to kill Elite/Boss enemies fast and can drive certain AoE DPS, such as Herta.

✦ However, she is still Single-Target, and will underperform in content that specifically favours AoE such as linked HP bosses.
column71:
As mentioned, gives Feixiao allies that attack frequently and/or good damage amplifier.

She works with typical Hypercarry setups or Dual DPS setups

column59:
Robin synergises well with allies that attack frequently. Her strong teamwide buffs also complement Feixiao's preference for Dual DPS setups. 

E2 lets each FUA from any ally charges 1 point of [Flying Aureus], greatly increasing the amount of Ultimates.

E1 and S1 are decent damage increases, though she has access to budget LCs.

E0S0 Feixiao with Robin is a great core, E2 Feixiao with E1 Robin is a menacing combo for vertical investment enjoyers.

column17:
Kit Overview: Single-Target Break DPS
Boothill is a strong ST Break DPS. With his skill, he forces an enemy and himself into Standoff State, increasing Boothill‘s DMG dealt and received, as well as enhancing his Basic Attack. By breaking or defeating the enemy while in Standoff, Boothill gains one [Pocket Trickshot], stacking up to a maximum of three. Each stack increases the Toughness Reduction and Break Damage dealt with his EBA.

His Ultimate applies Physical Weakness while dealing damage and delaying the enemy. Additionally, by using his technique, Boothill's first skill usage can also implant Physical Weakness.

Team Synergies:
✦ Break Damage amplifier: DEF Shred, Weakness-Break Efficiency, Break Effect% buff, etc.
✦ Action Advance or Speed buffer characters to let him take more turns.

Notes:
✦ Boothill's damage is miniscule against Immune to Toughness Reduction & Unbroken Enemies.
✦ Standoff State taunts the enemy, forcing them to attack Boothill and increasing their damage against him. This can make a strong sustain more necessary.
 
Tips:
✦ At the beginning of the battle, gain stacks faster by targeting weaker enemies first.
✦ Action advance support should be slightly slower than Boothill in battle.
column45:
✦ If you like the character, get them!

✦ Boothill makes for a great DPS against 
content with few enemies, dealing High ST
Break DMG. 

✦ He also has great and inexpensive 
4-Star / Standard banner support 
options,  making him easy enough to use on 
less invested accounts as well.
column71: Boothill can use any of the SuperBreak or Break supports, and he can also make great use of Action Advancers.

column59:
Fugue provides Exo-Toughness, and Dahlia provides a lot of great utilities and DMG to Break teams in general.

S1 provides BE%, DEF shred and SPD for the wearer. This is a decent LC for Boothill, but he also has a very strong alternative option.

E1 Boothill gives him 1 [Pocket Trickshot] when entering battle and 16% DEF shred. This is great for ramping up his [Pocket Trickshot] quickly.

E1S0 is a good stop, investments on supports are better aftwards.

column17:
Kit Overview: Single-Target FuA Specialist
When Dr. Ratio uses his Skill to deal damage and apply a debuff, there is a chance of launching a FUA attack afterward. The more debuffs the enemy has, the higher the chance to launch a FUA attack and the higher damage they will take from Dr. Ratio. If the enemy die before the FUA attack, he will attack a random enemy instead.

Dr. Ratio's Ultimate deal Single-target damage to an enemy and apply Wiseman's Folly. If this enemy is attacked by ally, Dr. Ratio will launch a FUA attack, this trigger up to 2 times.

Synergies:
✦ Debuffers with constant uptime are essential for consistently triggering FUA attacks. 
✦ Supports with strong damage amplification, action advance, energy are highly appreciated.

Notes:
✦ Light Cones that provide debuffs can be valuable on supports and sub-DPSes.
column45:
✦ If you like the character, get them!

✦ Dr. Ratio is a very strong option if help is needed with low-target content like MoC and AS. 

✦ He also has several flexible, inexpensive team options that include 4-star supports, making him simple to use even for an account with few 5-stars.
column71: Works in standard Dual DPS and Hypercarry setups, but wants at least 3 consistent debuffs active.

column59:
His signature Light Cone is strong, though its value can be diminished in teams with no Def Shred. He also has easy access to a budget option from Herta's Store.

His E1 provides decent damage, but is simply a statstick.

Topaz's signature Light Cone may be considered higher priority for his team if it lacks a third debuff.

column71: Vertical investment is required to ensure 3+ debuffs. Without at least 2 of: Topaz E1; Topaz  S1; Aventurine E2; and Aventurine S1, Topaz or Robin must be replaced with a debuffer.

column17:
Kit overview: Single-target FUA Sub-DPS
Topaz randomly inflicts [Proof of Debt] debuff on 1 target which increases Follow-Up Attack DMG taken, using Skill can reapply this debuff and let Numby attacks once. 

Numby appears on the action order and attack the target with said debuff, it gets advanced by 50% if its target is attacked by a FUA attack. All Topaz's attacks are considered FUA.

Topaz's Ultimate buffs Numby and it can be advanced by any attacks of other teammates for 2 turns.

Synergies:
✦ Topaz works well with any FUA DPS.
✦ Support units that can amplify team DMG, as Topaz damage contribution to the team is significant.
✦ Units that has frequent FUA attacks.

Notes:
✦ Topaz can be a SP neutral or SP positive sub-DPS as spamming skill is unnecessary.
✦ She can even be played as a hypercarry role but require good investment and supports to have decent results. Otherwise, using her in a sub-DPS role is recommended.
column45:
✦ If you like the character, get them!

✦ Topaz is a good ally option for most FUA DPS, but since she doesn't offer other specialized effects and her debuff is single target, she isn't high priority pull for most FUA DPS.
column71: Works in any team that has FUA DPS

column59: S1 provides good stat buff, can work for all FUA Hunt unit and make the wearer able to inflict a debuff: making the target take increased 24% Crit DMG.

column17:
Kit Overview: The Hunt unit that prefer small targets situations
Seele's Basic Attack deals minor ST damage, and action forward herself. Seele's Skill deals ST Damage, and increases her speed.

Her Ultimate deals greater ST Damage, and activates her buffed state.

Whenever any of Seele kills an enemy, she will trigger [Resurgence], entering her buffed state which increases her DMG% and Quantum RES PEN for 1 turn, then gives her an extra turn. During this extra turn, if Seele kills an enemy, she cannot trigger [Resurgence].

Team Synergies:
✦ Seele's kit is fairly basic, making most supports work well with her. 
✦ As Seele only has a singular hit in her Ultimate, it is advised that her supports can give her the Crit Rate needed to reach 100% (ie. Sunday, Sparkle(S1), Fu Xuan)

Notes:
✦ Allies off-turn DMG like Robin, Tribbie can steal kill and make Seele fail to trigger [Resurgence]

Tips:
✦ If Ultimate is ready to use, or it will be full after the use of BA/Skill, during the attack animation, activate Ultimate which let you use BA/Skill -> Ult -> 2 extra turns which let you gain more energy.
column45:
✦ If you like the character, get them!

✦ Seele's multiple actions can make her surprisingly viable in modes that aren't meant for ST DPSes, such as Pure Fiction, as she can kill small enemies quite quickly.

✦ Seele is very dependant on her match-up, wanting her target to have mobs on the side to activate the extra turns. Optimal performance require you to make use of her BA's Action Advance, being able to keep buff uptime and consistently gain Resurgence, which means her performance varies but can still clear well.
column71: Basically any hypercarry setup works for her

column59:
Seele's notable eidolons are E2 and E4, her peak investment stops at her Signature Light Cone. 

It's better to invest into supports as they tend to bring bigger boost to Seele's gameplay

column9: Use A for AA or SP

column17:
Kit Overview: ST DPS who can't take DMG
When using Yanqing's Skill, he deals ST damage and activates Soulsteel Sync, which last for one turn or disappear when Yanqing takes damage. 

Under Soulsteel Sync effect, Yanqing gains CR% and CDMG%, his attacks also has a fixed chance to unleash a FUA to the attacked enemy and a chance freeze them. Frozen enemy take additional damage on their turn. Yanqing also deals additional DMG to enemy with Ice weakness.

Upon using Yanqing's Ultimate, he gains massive amount of CR%; if Soulsteel Sync is activated, he also gains CDMG%, and unleash a ST attack.

Synergies:
✦ Supports that prevent Yanqing from taking DMG such as shielders.
✦ Supports that give damage amplification, action advance, energy, and so on are appreciated. However they also want to be SP positive.

Notes:
✦ In order to maintaing Soulsteel Sync all the time, Yanqing must not take damage and always spam Skill, which causes a heavy SP burden to the team.

Tips:
✦ Make sure Soulsteel Sync is active when Yanqing uses his Ultimate.
column45:
✦ If you like the character, get them!

✦ Yanqing can be obtained from Standard banner or from losing 50/50 so you do not need to specifically aim to get him.

✦ Yanqing is not considered a very strong DPS due to strict conditions and playstyle, and his scalings are rather low which is why he is not recommended to build and use. However if you like Yanqing, with good teams and supports, he can still perform well like any other DPS.
column71: Yanqing requires a shielder to prevent his buffs from disappearing among the sea of butterflies

column59:
Aventurine's chunky stackable shields have the highest chance of preventing Yanqing from getting hit.

E4 gives Yanqing a Ice RES PEN boost when his HP is above 80%.

E6 extends the buffs granted by Soulsteel Sync and his ultimate by a turn when an enemy is defeated with them.
